entries.title: Training Camp Is A Secret Intensive Course

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The summer training camp moves Class 1-A and Class 1-B into a remote, controlled site meant to accelerate combat and Quirk growth away from ordinary school routines.
content.injection: At the training camp, treat the remote forest site as an intensive U.A. training environment: exhausting, supervised, isolated, and not casually accessible to outsiders.
constraints: The camp is not a public event like the Sports Festival.
Students are still first-years under school authority.
antiLore: Do not portray the camp as a casual vacation or open civilian venue.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Wild Wild Pussycats Control The Camp Environment

context.label: Wild Wild Pussycats host training camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Wild Wild Pussycats are pro heroes responsible for camp supervision and terrain-based training, with rescue and search-oriented specialties that shape the setting.
content.injection: Use the Pussycats as active camp authorities with rescue, terrain, telepathic, and search utility rather than background chaperones.
constraints: Their presence makes the camp structured and supervised before the attack.
antiLore: Do not ignore the Pussycats when camp logistics, warnings, or evacuation matter.

entries.title: Kota Rejects Hero Culture From Personal Grief

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kota rejects heroes because his parents died as heroes, making him hostile to heroic ideals rather than simply rude or childish.
content.injection: Before Izuku saves Kota, portray Kota’s hostility as grief-driven rejection of hero culture, not generic bratty behavior.
constraints: He must not instantly admire U.A. students before his crisis with Muscular.
antiLore: Do not make Kota a cheerful hero fan at camp start.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

context.label: Quirk training intensifies under camp conditions

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The camp training targets each student’s Quirk limits and weaknesses through repeated physical strain, endurance, and specialized exercises.
content.injection: During camp training scenes, emphasize individualized Quirk strain and weakness-targeted drills instead of generic sparring.
constraints: Students are improving but remain first-years with limited stamina and incomplete control.
antiLore: Do not jump students to later-war mastery levels during this arc.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.fact: Class 1-B is present at the training camp, giving the attack wider stakes than a Class 1-A-only incident.
content.injection: At the camp, include Class 1-B as part of the training cohort and crisis environment; do not treat the forest attack as affecting only Class 1-A.
constraints: Class 1-B students have their own competence and reactions.
antiLore: Do not omit Class 1-B when the camp-wide evacuation or gas battle matters.

entries.title: Aizawa Keeps Camp Authority Blunt And Practical

context.label: Quirk training intensifies under camp conditions

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aizawa’s camp role is strict, pragmatic supervision; he focuses on survival, training value, and student accountability rather than reassurance.
content.injection: Use Aizawa as terse emergency authority at camp: practical, unsentimental, and protective through discipline rather than comfort.
constraints: He can be concerned without softening his teaching style.
antiLore: Do not make Aizawa casually permissive about student recklessness.

entries.title: Camp Location Does Not Prevent Villain Infiltration

context.label: Vanguard Action Squad attacks the camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The League finds the supposedly secret camp location, proving U.A.’s security assumptions are compromised.
content.injection: Once the Vanguard attacks, frame the camp breach as a serious security failure. Before the attack, do not let students know the League already found them.
constraints: The leak source should remain unresolved in this arc.
antiLore: Do not reveal the later traitor answer during the training camp.

entries.title: Pixie-Bob Terrain Beasts Are Training Obstacles

context.label: Wild Wild Pussycats host training camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.

content.fact: Pixie-Bob’s created earth beasts turn the forest route into Quirk-training resistance rather than normal hiking.
content.injection: During the arrival challenge, use Pixie-Bob’s terrain beasts as controlled training pressure, not actual villains.
constraints: The threat is artificial before the Vanguard attack.
antiLore: Do not confuse Pussycats training obstacles with League creatures.

entries.title: Kendo Shows Class 1-B Leadership Under Pressure

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.

content.fact: Kendo’s leadership and tactical judgment help Class 1-B respond to Mustard’s gas crisis.
content.injection: Use Kendo as a competent Class 1-B leader in the gas battle, coordinating rather than panicking.
constraints: Her role is practical leadership under pressure.
antiLore: Do not treat Class 1-B as passive victims during the camp attack.

entries.title: Students Are Split Across The Forest During The Attack

context.label: Vanguard Action Squad attacks the camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.

content.fact: The Vanguard attack scatters students across the forest, limiting communication and making local decisions matter.
content.injection: During the camp attack, avoid omniscient class coordination; students should act from partial information and local danger.

entries.title: Teachers Cannot Instantly Protect Every Student

context.label: Vanguard Action Squad attacks the camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The camp attack overwhelms adult coverage, forcing first-year students into dangerous survival decisions.
content.injection: Keep adult heroes limited by distance, simultaneous threats, and evacuation pressure; they cannot appear everywhere at once.
